name: hashcat (World's Fastest Password Cracker)

auth.notes: Offline password cracking; no auth required

performance.notes: GPU-accelerated; billions of passwords/second for MD5; GH100 GPU fastest

agent_readiness.notes: GPU-accelerated password hash cracking. hashcat -m HASH_TYPE -a ATTACK_MODE hashes.txt wordlist.txt. Hash types: 0=MD5, 100=SHA1, 1000=NTLM, 1800=sha512crypt, 22000=WPA-PBKDF2-PMKID+EAPOL. Attack modes: 0=dictionary, 1=combination, 3=brute-force mask, 6=hybrid. Rules: -r rockyou-30000.rule. Mask: ?u=uppercase, ?l=lowercase, ?d=digit, ?s=special. --show to display cracked. rockyou.txt + best64.rule = standard starting point. OpenCL/CUDA GPU acceleration.

gotchas: GPU required for practical cracking speed (CPU mode extremely slow)
Only crack hashes you are authorized to crack
Modern bcrypt/Argon2 hashes resist GPU acceleration
Cloud GPU instances expensive for long cracking sessions
best_when: Penetration test credential recovery from captured hashes
Password audit for corporate password policy enforcement
Offline NTLM/NTLMv2 hash cracking from network captures
avoid_when: When John the Ripper suffices for CPU-only environments
summary: hashcat is the world's fastest GPU-accelerated password cracker. Supports 300+ hash types (NTLM, SHA-1, bcrypt, WPA, etc.). Dictionary, mask (brute-force), combination, and hybrid attacks. Rules for word mangling (best64, rockyou-30000). OpenCL/CUDA GPU support. Standard tool for pentest credential recovery and password audits. Only crack authorized hashes.
